Issue: docs: clarify volumetric array axis order is WHD (X,Y,Z), not HWD
Decision: Update docstrings to use WHD / CWHD / NCWHD instead of HWD / CHWD / NCHWD
Rationale: Correct spatial axis conventions to match NIfTI/ITK reader outputs (WHD instead of HWD) for clarity and consistency
Author: aymuos15
Description: Volumetric array axis order in MONAI's NIfTI/ITK readers and related modules
Cost: Documentation-only changes; no runtime behavior affected
Risk: Potential confusion in existing code/doc mismatch (e.g., deepedit/transforms.py) if not addressed separately

Argument.author: aymuos15
Argument.argument: MONAI's NIfTI/ITK readers return ndarrays with axis 0 as columns/Width (X), axis 1 as rows/Height (Y), and axis 2 as Depth/slices (Z), which is WHD (X,Y,Z) order. This PR updates docstrings to reflect this convention (WHD / CWHD / NCWHD) instead of the previously incorrect HWD / CHWD / NCHWD.

Justification: The issue pertains to updating documentation conventions (docstrings) for axis order, which is not specific to ML lifecycle stages. Keywords like 'docs', 'docstrings', and 'Documentation-only changes' indicate this is a documentation update rather than an ML process change.
